Database reviewer note: CD19 CAR-T: PI: CRS grades 1-4; ICANS (neurological). Prolonged cytopenias post-infusion may last months. RT timing relative to CAR-T: no published evidence. Lymphodepletion conditioning (typically cyclophosphamide+fludarabine given 5-7 days prior) is itself RT-relevant. CNS RT post-CAR-T ICANS: extreme caution. Specialist (haematology + radiation oncology) planning essential. ASTRO 2024 pilot: split-course RT as bridging before CAR-T (after lymphodepletion) reported feasible in early data (Imber et al, NCT06104592); RT-as-bridging is distinct from concurrent RT during CRS. Post-CAR-T RT: pilot study ongoing (NCT04473937). No safety data extractable from abstracts at this time.

Database reviewer note: ATRA for APML. PI: differentiation syndrome (boxed); pseudotumour cerebri (raised ICP) - relevant for concurrent brain/cranial RT; QTc prolongation. Preclinical: ATRA + RT enhances tumour radioresponse via inflammatory macrophages / immune modulation (Rao 2021, Science Immunology); suggests tumour radiosensitisation rather than normal-tissue injury increase. NO clinical concurrent-RT toxicity study in APML. RT rarely used in contemporary APML management. APML-specific risks dominate: differentiation syndrome, bleeding (thrombocytopenia), leukocytosis, hepatotoxicity, ATO co-administration. Do not infer clinical safety from experimental models. Retain Insufficient evidence.
